The

Networks

convolutional
 
operations

local
 
spatial

global

computer
 
vision

image
 
classification,
 
object

and
 
segmentation.

1.
 
Image
 
Patching
 
and
 
Embedding:
 
This

and

input

x

height,

and

fixed-size
 
patches,

to
 
tokens
 
in
 
an
 
NLP

is
 
divided
 
into
 
non-overlapping
 
patches
 
(e.g.,
 
a
 
224x224
 
image
 
split
 
into
 
16x16
 
patches
 
yields
 
196
 
patches).
 
Each
 
patch
 
is
 
then
 
flattened
 
into
 
a
 
1D
 
vector
 
and
 
undergoes
 
a
 
learnable
 
linear
 
projection
 
into
 
a
 
higher-dimensional
 
embedding
 
space.
 
This
 
projection
 
allows
 
the
 
model
 
to
 
learn
 
richer
 
feature
 
representations
 
for
 
each
 
patch,
 
resulting
 
in
 
a
 
sequence
 
of
 
patch
 
embeddings,
 
each
 
representing
 
a
 
segment
 
of
 
the
 
image.
37
 
2.
 
Positional
 
Encoding:
 
Since
 
transformers
 
inherently
 
lack
 
the
 
ability
 
to
 
capture
 
the
 
spatial
 
order
 
of
 
input
 
sequences,
 
positional

are

to

the
 
spatial

the
 
original

encodings
 
are
 
added
 
to

embedding,

information
